q.en: Where and when was Daniel Boone born?
q.zh: 丹尼尔·布恩在哪里、何时出生？

options.en: In Kentucky in 1800
options.zh: 1800年，出生于肯塔基

options.en: In North Carolina in 1750
options.zh: 1750年，出生于北卡罗来纳

options.en: In Pennsylvania in 1734
options.zh: 1734年，出生于宾夕法尼亚

options.en: In Missouri in 1734
options.zh: 1734年，出生于密苏里

explain.en: The book says, "When Daniel Boone was born in 1734... He was born in Pennsylvania."
explain.zh: 书中说：“丹尼尔·布恩1734年出生时……他出生在宾夕法尼亚。”

q.en: The book says Daniel "walked silently" even though he was "tall and sturdy." What does this show?
q.zh: 书中说丹尼尔虽然“高大结实”，却“走路悄无声息”。这说明了什么？

options.en: He was a skilled hunter who could move without being noticed
options.zh: 他是个技术高超的猎手，能不被察觉地移动

options.en: He was lost
options.zh: 他迷路了

options.en: He was afraid of animals according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他害怕动物（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He was lazy
options.zh: 他很懒

explain.en: The book says he "became an extremely skilled hunter. Though he was tall and sturdy, he learned to walk silently," showing his woodland skill.
explain.zh: 书中说他“成了技艺极高的猎手。虽然高大结实，他却学会了悄无声息地走路”，体现了他的林中本领。

q.en: Why did the fort need salt so badly?
q.zh: 为什么堡垒那么急需盐？

options.en: Because they wanted to build a wall according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 因为他们想筑一堵墙（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Because they used it for light
options.zh: 因为他们用它来照明

options.en: Because salt made the water sweet
options.zh: 因为盐能让水变甜

options.en: Because before refrigerators, salt was the only way to preserve (cure) food
options.zh: 因为在冰箱出现之前，盐是保存（腌制）食物的唯一办法

explain.en: The book says, "Before refrigerators, the only way to preserve food was to cure it with salt," so salt was essential and they boiled a salt-water spring to make it.
explain.zh: 书中说：“在冰箱出现之前，保存食物的唯一办法是用盐腌制。”所以盐必不可少，他们把咸水泉煮沸来制盐。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in telling Daniel Boone's life?
q.zh: 作者讲述丹尼尔·布恩一生的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To entertain with a fantasy
options.zh: 为了用一个奇幻故事娱乐读者

options.en: To inform readers about a famous frontier explorer who opened Kentucky to settlers
options.zh: 为了向读者介绍一位开拓肯塔基、让定居者进入西部的著名边境探险家

options.en: To explain only how to make jerky according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了只讲如何做肉干（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To teach how to farm
options.zh: 为了教人如何耕种

explain.en: The introduction says Daniel Boone "brought American settlers into those lands for the first time," and the book traces his real life as a frontier explorer.
explain.zh: 引言说丹尼尔·布恩“第一次把美国定居者带进了那些土地”，全书按真实生平讲述这位边境探险家。

q.en: How did Daniel Boone escape after being captured?
q.zh: 丹尼尔·布恩被俘后是如何逃脱的？

options.en: He was rescued by the British according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他被英国人救出（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He slipped away from the group at the salt springs and ran back to the fort in five days
options.zh: 他在咸水泉边从队伍中溜走，用五天跑回堡垒

options.en: He flew away
options.zh: 他飞走了

options.en: He swam across the ocean
options.zh: 他游过了大洋

explain.en: At the salt springs, "Daniel managed to slip away... He ran all the way back to the fort... The trip took five days."
explain.zh: 在咸水泉边，“丹尼尔设法溜走……一路跑回堡垒……旅程花了五天。”

q.en: In the glossary, what does the word "mourned" mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，“mourned（哀悼）”是什么意思？

options.en: Built
options.zh: 建造

options.en: Grieved or felt sad, especially for someone who has died
options.zh: 悲伤或难过，尤指为逝者

options.en: Celebrated according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 庆祝（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Hunted
options.zh: 打猎

explain.en: The glossary defines mourned as "grieved or felt sad, especially for someone who has died (p. 9)." Daniel mourned his oldest son.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 mourned 定义为“悲伤或难过，尤指为逝者”（第9页）。丹尼尔为长子之死而哀悼。

q.en: How did the settlers build the fort for protection?
q.zh: 定居者是怎样建造堡垒来防御的？

options.en: Tough log houses in a tight square facing inward, with back walls forming a high fence wall
options.zh: 结实的原木屋围成紧密的正方形、朝内，后墙连成高高的栅栏墙

options.en: A wall made of salt
options.zh: 一堵盐做的墙

options.en: A single cabin in the woods according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 树林里一间小木屋（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Tents around a fire
options.zh: 火堆旁的几顶帐篷

explain.en: They "built tough log houses in a tight square, all facing inward. They extended the back walls... into high log fences. The fences formed a solid wall around the settlement."
explain.zh: 他们“建起结实的原木屋，围成紧密的正方形、全部朝内。把后墙延长……变成高高的原木栅栏。栅栏在定居点四周形成一道坚固的墙。”

q.en: Why does the author include Daniel's later move to Missouri?
q.zh: 作者为什么要写到丹尼尔后来迁往密苏里？

options.en: To explain the Louisiana Purchase according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了解释路易斯安那购地（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To say he became a king
options.zh: 为了说他成了国王

options.en: To show that nothing could keep him away from the forest, even in old age
options.zh: 为了说明即便上了年纪，也没什么能让他离开森林

options.en: To describe schools in St. Louis
options.zh: 为了描写圣路易斯的学校

explain.en: The book says, "nothing could keep Daniel Boone away from the forest, not even old age," and he moved to Missouri for its wilderness, living there the rest of his days.
explain.zh: 书中说：“没什么能让他离开森林，连年迈也不能。”他为了密苏里的荒野迁居那里，并在那里度过余生。

q.en: What happened to Daniel's family while he was thought dead?
q.zh: 在大家以为丹尼尔已死时，他的家人怎么样了？

options.en: They had moved back to North Carolina
options.zh: 他们已经搬回了北卡罗来纳

options.en: They joined the British
options.zh: 他们投靠了英国人

options.en: They moved to Detroit
options.zh: 他们搬到了底特律

options.en: They went to live with the Native Americans
options.zh: 他们去和印第安人一起生活了

explain.en: When Daniel returned, "his friends welcomed him as though he had returned from the dead. But sadly, Daniel's family had given him up for dead and moved back to North Carolina."
explain.zh: 丹尼尔回来时，“朋友们像迎接死而复生的人一样欢迎他。但遗憾的是，家人以为他死了，已经搬回北卡罗来纳。”

q.en: According to the book, what made Daniel Boone valuable to the Native Americans who captured him?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，是什么让俘获他的印第安人觉得丹尼尔·布恩很有价值？

options.en: His gold
options.zh: 他的金子

options.en: His fear of water
options.zh: 他怕水

options.en: His written language according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他的文字（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: His rare skill in the forest, unusual for a European
options.zh: 他在森林中罕见的本领，对一个欧洲人来说很不寻常

explain.en: On the way to Chillicothe, "the Native Americans became impressed with Daniel's skill in the forest. This skill was rare in a European. Such a man was very valuable."
explain.zh: 在去奇利科西的路上，“印第安人对丹尼尔的森林本领印象深刻。这种本领在欧洲人身上很少见。这样的一个人很有价值。”

prompt.en: Daniel Boone loved the wilderness and kept exploring even when life was hard. What is a place or activity you love so much that you would work hard to be there?
prompt.zh: 丹尼尔·布恩热爱荒野，即便生活艰难也不断去探索。有没有一个地方或一项活动让你如此热爱，以至于愿意努力去接近它？

en: Daniel learned forest skills as a boy and loved the woods all his life.
He faced danger and loss but still returned to the wilderness.
Moving west to Kentucky and later Missouri showed his love of open land.
Liking the pure air of the forest, he went there just to breathe.
zh: 丹尼尔小时候就学会了森林本领，一生都热爱树林。
他虽遭遇危险与失去，仍回到荒野。
向西迁往肯塔基、后来到密苏里，都体现了他对开阔土地的喜爱。
他喜欢森林里纯净的空气，常去那里只为呼吸。
